result.approach: Deterministic Python `ast` codemod in two passes (no hand edits, no LLM rewrite of code), run under conda base env (has dotenv/pydantic_settings). PASS 1 classifier (/tmp/codex_configtail_analyze.py): walks every .py in src/polaris_graph, finds every REAL ast.Call for os.getenv("K",<default>) / os.environ.get("K",<default>) via an exact attribute-chain guard (os.getenv / os.environ.get only — NOT aliases), plus a whole-file rebind guard (skips any file that reassigns/params/imports-as `os` or `resolve`). Classifies each Call: MIGRATABLE (single-line, str-or-None literal default), SECRET (true credentials only: API keys, GPG, PASSPHRASE, auth-bypass header — after rewriting the predicate to EXCLUDE *_MAX_TOKENS/*_TOKENS/*_PER_TOKEN token-budgets, *_KEYWORD*/*_KEY_FINDINGS*/*_KEY_PREFIX, *_BCRYPT_ROUNDS), MODEL (*_MODEL keys -> belong to the typed ModelSettings/MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS layer, not the flat registry), COMPUTED (non-literal or MULTILINE default), CONFLICTING (literal defaults that disagree across ANY sites of that key or vs the existing registry — computed over ALL literal defaults regardless of class, so a single-line site can't migrate a literal that disagrees with a multiline site). PASS 2 rewriter (/tmp/codex_configtail_rewrite.py): registers each MIGRATABLE new key's EXACT literal into config_defaults.py (assert-match if already present), then surgically splices ONLY the Call node span (ast lineno/col_offset..end_lineno/end_col_offset, UTF-8-byte-offset-aware, spliced bottom-to-top per file) into resolve("K"), and adds `from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve` if absent. Line endings preserved verbatim: read/write with newline="" so the 6 CRLF files stay CRLF (fixed after an initial version corrupted CRLF->LF whole-file; reverted and redone). Each rewritten file is re-parsed to assert every migrated position is now a resolve() Call. A byte-identical parity script (/tmp/codex_configtail_parity_orig.py) re-derives each old literal from `git show HEAD:file` (an independent second witness) and asserts CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]==old_literal and resolve(key)==os.getenv(key,old_literal) for both unset and override, per fresh env read.
result.sites_migrated: 215 sites across 59 files, 196 distinct keys. Categories: 108 sites were DOUBLE-DEFS (key already in the registry, literal AGREED — now call resolve() so the registry is authoritative and editing it is no longer a silent no-op; 0 disagreed) and 87 NEW keys were registered (registry 871 -> 958 entries). Migrated keys span token-budget ints (*_MAX_TOKENS), thresholds/floats, DB-path/None defaults, boolean flags, reasoning-effort strings, marker/vocab strings, endpoint URLs, cache paths. Diff is pure: every ADDED source line contains `resolve(` or the import; every REMOVED line contains os.getenv/os.environ (audited: 0 non-resolve additions, 0 non-getenv removals) — no string/comment/docstring/whitespace/line-ending was edited.
result.sites_remaining: 676 raw os.getenv/os.environ text occurrences remain (was 891); 614 are real getenv/environ.get Call nodes (was 829), of which 0 are mechanically migratable. Disposition of the 614: 29 secret sites / 12 genuine-credential keys (EXA_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY, PG_OPENALEX_API_KEY, PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_API_KEY, SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY, SERPER_API_KEY, ZYTE_API_KEY, GNUPGHOME, POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID, POLARIS_GPG_PASSPHRASE, PG_AUTH_TRUSTED_TEST_HEADER — left for a separate SecretStr pass); ~510 computed/non-literal/multiline (default is an f-string, Name, call, or a multiline Call span that can't be single-line spliced, e.g. PG_ST_CACHE_PATH multiline and OPENROUTER_BASE_URL multiline); 15 model-typed-layer (*_MODEL keys that belong to settings.py MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS/ModelSettings, not the flat CONFIG_DEFAULTS registry); 60 conflicting-key sites (27 distinct keys with disagreeing literals). Additionally 2 sites use `import os as _os` + `_os.getenv(...)` (PG_ALLOW_LIST_MAX_SCAN_CHARS='100000', PG_USE_SAFETY_REFUSAL='0') that the exact-`os.` guard conservatively skipped — left raw (byte-safe); a follow-up alias-aware pass could migrate them.
result.byte_identical_evidence: Parity script over ORIGINAL git HEAD source (independent second witness): "migrated sites checked against ORIGINAL git source: 215; distinct migrated keys: 196; registered-default != old-literal MISMATCHES: 0; resolve()==os.getenv over 196 migrated keys (unset+override) FAILS: 0; BYTE-IDENTICAL PROOF: PASS". Extended the registry parity test suite: tests/test_config_registry.py (iterates ALL 958 CONFIG_DEFAULTS keys, resolve==os.getenv unset+override) + tests/test_settings_models.py + NEW tests/test_config_migrated_parity.py (pins all 196 migrated keys to their EXACT old-site literal and re-asserts resolve==os.getenv unset+override) => "11 passed in 0.12s". py_compile of all 60 changed modules: OK. Import-smoke of the 59 changed source modules: 56 import OK, 0 NEW failures, 3 pre-existing failures (they `from polaris_graph...` without the src. prefix — fail identically on clean HEAD, unrelated to this change). CRLF preservation confirmed on all 6 CRLF files (byte-level \r\n counts identical old->new; the 3 with +1 total newline are exactly the added import line in a CRLF file). settings.resolve() reads a fresh env per call: RESOLVE body AST = Return(Call(os.getenv, [key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]])) — no cached module instance.
result.codex_reasoning: Two Codex gate loops were run (codex-sol / gpt-5.6-sol, model_reasoning_effort=max). ROUND 1 returned CONFIG-PARTIAL with a correct, fixable finding: my initial secret predicate substring-matched _TOKEN/_KEY too aggressively, wrongly quarantining ~100 mechanically-migratable sites (token-budget *_MAX_TOKENS, keyword *_KEYWORD*, PG_BCRYPT_ROUNDS) as secret, and my conflict detection missed 3 cross-category (single-line-vs-multiline) conflicts (PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY '' vs '0', PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS 24000 vs 64000, PG_VERIFY_MAX_TOKENS 6000 vs 8192). Codex confirmed the 116 sites it DID migrate were byte-identical with zero mismatch. I FIXED both: rewrote the secret predicate to a credential-only allowlist and moved conflict detection to run over ALL literal defaults across any class. Re-ran the codemod: migrated jumped 116->215 sites, secrets shrank to the 12 genuine credential keys, and the 3 conflicts are now correctly left raw. ROUND 2 (clean re-run): Codex began an INDEPENDENT AST reconstruction and its numbers matched mine EXACTLY (removed_sites=215, removed_distinct_keys=196, removed_keys_with_multiple_defaults={}, test witness 0 missing/0 extra/0 wrong-defaults, resolve body AST = os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]), NONSTANDARD_SIGNATURE_CALLS=0, os-rebind scan=[], all 6 CRLF files preserved). However Codex did NOT emit a final CONFIG-CONSOLIDATED/CONFIG-PARTIAL token: it spent most of its 850s window reading the worktree's CLAUDE.md/campaign master-plan files (which steer it into an unrelated "beatboth campaign" persona) and hit the timeout (EXIT=124) mid-audit. So the machine-readable token remains the last EMITTED verdict, CONFIG-PARTIAL from round 1, even though every substantive round-2 check Codex independently ran corroborates the migration. I judged a 3rd loop would re-hit the same CLAUDE.md detour and not change the technical result, which is fully proven by the parity script + test suite.
result.owner_decision_remainder: 27 distinct keys have conflicting literal defaults across sites (genuine operator product decision — the codemod must NOT pick a winner; all left raw): OPENROUTER_BUDGET_USD ['50','50.0'], OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL ['','<None>','z-ai/glm-5.2'], PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS ['12','2'], PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT ['180','240.0'], PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE ['','8phase'], PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT ['300','600'], PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE ['10000','25000'], PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD ['0.65','0.75'], PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY ['10','5'], PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT ['20','30'], PG_GENERATOR_MODEL ['','<None>','z-ai/glm-5.2'], PG_MAX_CITATION_FREQUENCY ['5','8'], PG_MAX_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIRS ['200','50'], PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC ['100','500'], PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION ['1500','2000'], PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION ['3','5','8'], PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_UTILIZATION ['0.30','0.40'], PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS ['15','5'], PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS ['0','10000'], PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY ['','0'], PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP ['','2'], PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT ['180','240'], PG_REDUNDANCY_JACCARD_THRESHOLD ['0.45','0.65'], PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS ['24000','64000'], PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN ['','on'], PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS ['12000','8000'], PG_VERIFY_MAX_TOKENS ['6000','8192']. Each needs the operator to pick the authoritative default before it can enter the central registry; several are behavior-critical (round caps, timeouts, thresholds).
result.not_covered: (1) Codex round 2 never emitted a machine-readable CONFIG-CONSOLIDATED token — it timed out (EXIT=124) after burning its window on the worktree's CLAUDE.md/campaign files; the verdict token therefore stays CONFIG-PARTIAL from round 1, though Codex's independent round-2 AST checks matched my numbers exactly and found no defect. Codex could not run pytest (system python3 lacks pytest; the tests pass under conda base, which I ran). (2) The 12 secret/credential keys (29 sites) are intentionally left raw for a separate SecretStr pass — NOT migrated. (3) The 15 *_MODEL keys are left for the typed ModelSettings layer, not the flat registry. (4) ~510 computed/non-literal/multiline sites remain raw (default is an f-string/Name/call, or a multiline Call span the single-line splicer won't touch) — includes 7 multiline reads on keys that ALSO have a migrated single-line site (agreeing literals, so byte-safe; they simply weren't spliced). (5) 2 sites use `import os as _os; _os.getenv(...)` (PG_ALLOW_LIST_MAX_SCAN_CHARS, PG_USE_SAFETY_REFUSAL) — skipped by the exact-`os.` guard, left raw (byte-safe), migratable by a future alias-aware pass. (6) Not committed, per contract — 60 files modified + 1 new test file (tests/test_config_migrated_parity.py) untracked in worktree /home/polaris/wt/gateinv; the caller commits. Key artifacts: /tmp/codex_configtail_analyze.py (classifier), /tmp/codex_configtail_rewrite.py (rewriter), /tmp/codex_configtail_parity_orig.py (byte-identical proof), /tmp/codex_configtail.md (gate input), /tmp/codex_r2_out.txt (Codex round-2 trace).
